# opnsense_cron_job (Resource)

Cron jobs allow scheduling commands to run at specified times.

## Example Usage

```terraform
// Run firmware update check every day at 4am
resource "opnsense_cron_job" "firmware_check" {
  minutes = "0"
  hours   = "4"
  command = "firmware poll"
  description = "Daily firmware update check"
}

// Reload unbound every 6 hours, weekdays only
resource "opnsense_cron_job" "unbound_reload" {
  minutes  = "0"
  hours    = "*/6"
  weekdays = "1-5"
  command  = "unbound restart"
  description = "Periodic Unbound DNS reload"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `command` (String) Command to execute. Must be a command key known to OPNsense (e.g. `firmware poll`, `unbound restart`).
- `description` (String) Description for this cron job.
- `hours` (String) Hour(s) to run (cron expression, e.g. `0` or `*/6`).
- `minutes` (String) Minute(s) to run (cron expression, e.g. `0` or `*/15`).

### Optional

- `days` (String) Day(s) of month to run (cron expression, e.g. `*` or `1`).
- `enabled` (Boolean) Enable this cron job. Defaults to `true`.
- `months` (String) Month(s) to run (cron expression, e.g. `*` or `1,6`).
- `parameters` (String) Optional parameters to pass to the command.
- `weekdays` (String) Day(s) of week to run (cron expression, e.g. `*` or `1-5`).
- `who` (String) User to run the command as. Defaults to `root`.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) UUID of the cron job.
